threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a ‘cabbie’ instead of just ‘taxi’. The locals know it’s all about the black cabs, mate.
Caught the N3 last night and the driver was blasting 90s R&B. One guy started a mini dance party in the back, but the rest just rolled their eyes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids playing footie while their parents debated the latest BVG strike over coffee at the corner café.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a döner shop that opens at 3 AM, serving fresh bread and the best late-night chats with locals. No other city gets this.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local when they’re the only one to instinctively brace for a jolt on the BART platform before the train even arrives.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 bus yesterday, and a guy serenaded us with a sax solo. Only in San Francisco would a commute come with an unexpected concert.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every square in Gràcia is just a photo op, but locals know they’re vibrant community spaces where the real conversations happen.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newcomer by their enthusiasm for trying to pronounce 'xampanyet'—it's almost a rite of passage here in Gràcia.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the buses run on time. Locals have mastered the art of showing up 20 minutes late and pretending it was planned.
Last night on the 196, two lads debated the best chicken shops in Brixton. Turns out, even at 2 AM, they wouldn’t recommend anything but Etta's.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a hipster brewing coffee while a group of elderly folks played Skat in the park—definitely feels like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can hop on a tram, and within 10 minutes, be in a completely different vibe—first a techno rave, then a cozy café, all without changing routes.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a real local by how they know to avoid the bus stop by 16th and Mission after 5 PM—it's like a magnet for delays and drama.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full suit running after a bus, only to trip and fall into a puddle—still laughing as he got up. Classic SF chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm is in the small plazas where locals sip vermut and gossip over bocadillos.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still stare at the metro maps like they've never seen a line change before, looking around as if the locals will magically appear to guide them.
